articles.headlineTranslated: I thought it was hay fever... Measles, of all things. Early symptoms closely resembled hay fever; dangerous self-diagnosis. Doctors warn.
articles.summary: Measles outbreaks in Japan are driven by imported cases and the virus’s high transmissibility. Nationwide reports rose through early 2025, with 119 measles patients from January to mid-May, median age 24; 20s accounted for 34%, followed by 30s–40s; 79% had incomplete or unknown two-dose vaccination histories. A February Tokyo Dome concert attendee was diagnosed with modified measles, underscoring adult vulnerability. Guidance from clinicians in Kanagawa Prefecture (Sagamihara) emphasizes measles’ airborne, droplet, and contact transmission; infectiousness can persist after the patient leaves spaces like airports, trains, event venues, shopping centers, and medical facilities. Incubation is about 10–12 days, with fever, cough, runny nose, and red eyes; early symptoms can resemble hay fever, risking delayed diagnosis. Ongoing outbreaks stem from resumed international travel, overseas measles activity, and prior WHO elimination in 2015; vaccination remains the primary prevention strategy.

articles.summary: Measles: fourth infection in Kagoshima Prefecture in 2026, confirmed in Kagoshima City. The patient is a man in his 30s who developed fever on March 4 and a rash on March 7; he visited a medical facility on March 11. The prefecture’s genetic testing returned positive for measles on March 12. Measles spreads via droplets and direct contact and is highly contagious. The patient reportedly had contact with an unspecified large number of people at a shopping mall in Kagoshima City on the evening of March 11 (roughly 8–9 p.m.). Authorities urge individuals with suspected symptoms to contact a medical facility in advance before visiting to manage potential exposure and prevent further transmission. The report provides no details on the infection source or travel history.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles cases: 15 more confirmed in Tokyo; may have come into contact with an unspecified number of people at Hello Work Shibuya.
articles.summary: 15 new measles cases confirmed in Tokyo between 7–12 March 2026, bringing the year’s total to 27 and nearing the 2019 peak of 124. Vaccination remains protective; officials urge unvaccinated residents to get vaccinated. A Tokyo resident in her 30s infected with measles is suspected of having contacted an unspecified number of people at Hello Work Shibuya (Shibuya Public Employment Security Office). She visited the 2nd-floor Career Counseling window and the 3rd-floor Employment Insurance Benefits Division on 5 March from 16:00–16:20. She developed fever, cough, and rash from 6 March and was laboratory-confirmed with measles on 9 March. Vaccination history is unknown; no recent international travel. Authorities advise others who visited Hello Work Shibuya during that window to seek medical care in advance if ill and to avoid using public transport, following guidance.

articles.summary: In Nagano Prefecture, influenza cases per medical institution stood at 29.08 for the week ending March 8, down 8.08 from the prior week, marking a third consecutive weekly decline. Measles cases raise concern: a girl under 10 in Nagano City was diagnosed with measles as of March 7. Measles is highly contagious with an incubation period up to three weeks, presenting fever, cough, and rash. On March 6, a measles patient confirmed outside the prefecture traveled by JR Shinano Limited Express from Shiojiri Station to Nagoya Station. The prefecture urges anyone with suspected measles symptoms to contact a medical facility in advance and seek prompt evaluation and treatment.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles cases surge year-on-year; overseas-origin viral infections suspected | Weekly Sato Koichi's Blog!
articles.summary: Measles cases surge in Japan, with 87 infections reported through March 4, 2026, far above 9 at the same point last year. Tokyo Metropolis and Aichi Prefecture are among affected areas; officials warn many cases are imported from overseas, with about 15 infections (roughly 17%) presumed acquired abroad, notably in Indonesia and other Southeast Asian countries. Japan remains in a measles-elimination status, suggesting additional domestic cases are likely imported. The Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare has issued notices to prefectures to alert travelers and encourage reporting of travel history, urging preventive vaccination for those traveling to outbreak regions or in contact with vulnerable individuals. Measles is highly contagious; basic measures may be insufficient. Emphasized is routine vaccination at ages 1 and 5–6, and adults should verify vaccination history before travel or exposure. Dr. Nobuhiko Okabe of Kawasaki City Health Safety Research Institute warns of rapid spread and the need to prevent domestic re-establishment.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles cases reported in succession: a teenage male in Kagoshima City and a woman in her 40s from outside the prefecture.
articles.summary: As of March 10, 2026, Kagoshima Prefecture and Kagoshima City confirmed two measles cases: a teenage male in Kagoshima City and a woman in her 40s from outside the prefecture. Both were exposed to multiples during the infectious period. The teen’s laboratory-confirmed infection (reported March 9) is linked to a prior case confirmed February 6; between February 28 and March 6 he attended a skills test and visited commercial facilities in the city. The woman’s infection is an imported case with overseas travel during the incubation period; she entered the prefecture on March 7 by a friend’s car and was reported March 9, with no use of public transit or local facilities. Public Health authorities urge medical consultation if measles is suspected and to pursue vaccination; the prefecture indicates two to three measles cases have been confirmed in 2026 so far.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles cases in Tokyo reach 12 so far this year... Tokyo Medical Association calls for vaccination, "Herd immunity is declining."
articles.summary: Measles cases in Tokyo reach 12 by early March 2026, prompting the Tokyo Medical Association to emphasize vaccination as herd immunity declines. Tokyo’s measles counts have varied over the past decade, with a 2019 peak of 124 cases, zero cases in 2021–22 during the COVID-19 period, then 34 in the previous year, and 12 by early March 2026, a pace well above 2025. The virus causes fever, cough, and runny nose after about a 10-day incubation, followed by fever above 39°C and a rash; it spreads via airborne, droplet, and contact transmission, with about 1 in 1,000 infections fatal. Vaccination markedly lowers infection risk. Notably, several cases involved exposure to many people in major hospitals and other public facilities. Doctors urge those with suspected measles to contact clinics before visiting and to separate suspected patients in care settings.

articles.summary: Measles outbreak in Kagoshima Prefecture: The third case of the year confirmed in a woman in her 40s who lives outside the prefecture. She traveled on March 6 from Shiojiri Station, Nagano Prefecture, by JR to Hakata Station, then by a friend's car to Kagoshima. Fever began March 7; rash appeared March 8. She visited Kawaso Public Health Center facility on March 8, and genotype testing confirmed infection on March 9. The prefecture urges anyone who used the same transport during the same time frame to seek medical care if fever or rash develops within 21 days. Measles is highly contagious via droplets or contact. Earlier in March, measles was confirmed in Kagoshima City, and a teenage male case was reported on March 9 as part of the ongoing outbreak.

articles.headlineTranslated: One measles case confirmed in Nagano City; a girl under 10 years old with overseas travel history; visited local restaurants and other establishments.
articles.summary: One measles case confirmed in Nagano City, Japan. A girl under 10 years old, unvaccinated, with overseas travel history during the potential infectious period, has been identified by the Nagano City Public Health Center (announced March 8). The patient developed fever and cough, followed by a rash; laboratory confirmation via genetic testing was positive on March 7. The infection route has not been identified. The exposure window was February 28, 11:30 a.m. to February 29, 00:30, during which she reportedly visited local restaurants and other establishments in the city. Public health authorities are conducting investigations and contact tracing as needed to identify and manage any exposures.

articles.summary: Measles confirmed in Kagoshima City, Japan, in a woman in her 50s. Laboratory confirmation by prefectural tests followed onset of fever and rash on Feb 26; she was hospitalized Mar 1 and the case was officially confirmed on Mar 6. The infection route remains unknown, but she had contact in late January–late February with someone who had traveled to Tokyo. No exposure to a large number of people during the infectious period; close relatives are under health observation. Authorities note measles activity in major urban areas and urge vaccination and other countermeasures. Measles spreads via airborne, droplet, and contact transmission and is classified as Category 5 under Japan’s Infectious Diseases Control Law. Ongoing monitoring of contacts is in place to prevent further spread.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles-infected Tokyo resident in his 50s may have come into contact with an unspecified number of people at Tokyo Metropolitan Bokuto Hospital on the 4th.
articles.summary: Measles infection confirmed in a Tokyo resident in his 50s, with possible exposure to an unspecified number of people at Tokyo Metropolitan Bokuto Hospital (Sumida Ward) on March 4. The patient developed fever on February 26 and visited the hospital’s main building, first-floor general reception, around 1:30–1:40 pm on March 4 for testing. A PCR test on March 5 confirmed measles. He exhibited fever, conjunctival injection, and rash and remains hospitalized in a moderate condition. He has no recent international travel history and was not vaccinated against measles. The Tokyo Metropolitan Government urges anyone who visited the hospital at the same time to monitor for symptoms, avoid using public transportation if unwell, and seek medical care following guidance. The report highlights potential nosocomial exposure and the need for public health precautions.

articles.summary: Two new measles infections confirmed in Aichi Prefecture, Japan: a female high school student in Toyokawa City and a man in his 30s in Chiryu City. As of 2026-03-05, Aichi has recorded 23 measles cases this year. The Toyokawa student attends a prefectural high school in Higashi-Mikawa where 15 students have been infected this year. She developed fever and related symptoms on February 28 and was diagnosed with measles at a hospital. The Chiryu City resident developed symptoms on February 26; there was no known contact with a measles patient and no overseas travel history. The prefecture urges anyone with suspected measles symptoms to seek care at their nearest medical facility. The announcement highlights ongoing local transmission within the prefecture and the importance of early diagnosis and isolation to curb spread.

articles.summary: Influenza in Fukuoka Prefecture has fallen for two consecutive weeks, with the week ending March 1 reporting an average of 25.27 influenza patients per medical facility, down to 0.59 of the previous week. Despite the decline, influenza warnings have been in effect for 15 consecutive weeks, prompting ongoing infection-control advisories. No new measles infections were reported. The prefecture urged continued precautions, including handwashing and cough etiquette. No laboratory-confirmation details or information on imported or exported cases are provided. Data are based on medical-institution reports through March 1. The report underscores persistent outbreak status in Fukuoka as of March 5, 2026.

articles.headlineTranslated: Teenage boy who returned to Narita from New Zealand contracted measles; eighth case in Chiba Prefecture this year (Chiba TV).
articles.summary: Measles in Chiba Prefecture, Japan: A teenage male resident who returned from New Zealand contracted measles. He arrived at Narita Airport from Auckland International Airport on February 27, developed fever and rash, and sought care at a local clinic and Narita Red Cross Hospital. Genetic testing confirmed measles on March 1; he is recovering. As of 2026-03-05, this is the eighth measles case in Chiba Prefecture this year. Measles is highly contagious and airborne. Authorities urge individuals not fully vaccinated (two-dose routine immunization) or with unknown vaccination histories to consult their primary care physician and consider vaccination to prevent further spread. The case reflects imported transmission from international travel, with ongoing surveillance and contact tracing as needed.

articles.headlineTranslated: Number of influenza patients in Nagano Prefecture has exceeded the alert threshold for four consecutive weeks, despite a decline.
articles.summary: Influenza activity in Nagano Prefecture remained above the alert threshold for a fourth consecutive week, with 37.16 influenza cases per sentinel site in the week ending March 1, down 4.80 from the prior week but still above the 30-case alert level. By health‑office jurisdiction, Matsumoto reported 63.00, Iida 49.33, and Ueda 46.80 cases per sentinel; nine regions exceeded the threshold. Educational facilities recorded 2 closures, 20 grade-level suspensions, and 95 class suspensions. Norovirus gastroenteritis rose to 9.82 per sentinel (+0.64), with Matsumoto City 17.67 and Nagano City 16.40 per sentinel. COVID-19 declined to 2.74 per sentinel (−1.02); Nagano 6.25 and Hokushin (North Shinano) 5.00 per sentinel. With spring break and more international travel, authorities urged pre-travel measles vaccination considerations and post-return health monitoring.

articles.summary: Measles diagnosed among attendees of Vaundy’s February 14 Tokyo Dome concert (Vaundy DOME TOUR 2026 'SILENCE') is reported by the artist’s official site as of 2026-03-04. Affected attendees were diagnosed with modified measles, an infection occurring in individuals with insufficient immunity; symptoms are typically milder but the contagious risk persists for those nearby. The illness is linked to the Tokyo Dome show in Tokyo; the incubation period is about 10–12 days, up to 21 days. Attendees who experience fever, rash, cough, runny nose, or red eyes by March 7 are urged to contact a medical facility by phone in advance, wear a mask, and seek prompt care. The statement notes that events will continue under government guidance to ensure public safety. No case counts were disclosed. Key topics: measles, Tokyo, Japan, public health advisory, modified measles, incubation period.

articles.summary: On 2026-03-02, Japan’s Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW) expert panel approved adding a preventive dosing indication to the oral COVID-19 antiviral Zokoba for individuals at infection risk, such as household contacts, to prevent onset if exposure occurs. If finalized, Zokoba would become the world’s first COVID-19 oral drug with a preventive use. The panel noted that preventive dosing already exists for several influenza antivirals. In the same meeting, the committee approved manufacturing and marketing of a three-in-one measles–rubella–mumps (MMR) vaccine, i.e., combining MR with mumps vaccine. These decisions signal regulatory steps to broaden COVID-19 prevention options in Japan while expanding routine vaccination choices through an MMR product.}}{

articles.headline: ゾコーバ「予防投与」を承認へ　新型コロナ発症前に、世界初
articles.headlineTranslated: Zocoba to be approved for 'prophylactic administration' before onset of COVID-19, the world's first.
articles.summary: Japan's Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare expert panel on 2026-03-02 approved adding 'prophylactic administration' as an indication for the oral COVID-19 antiviral Zocoba, enabling pre-exposure or exposure-based use to prevent illness in people at risk after potential infection. If approved through standard procedures, it would be the world's first oral COVID-19 medicine with a prophylaxis indication. Prophylaxis would target individuals with close contact, such as cohabitants of confirmed cases. The panel noted that for influenza, preventive antiviral use already exists. In the same meeting, authorities also approved manufacturing and sale of a three-component measles–mumps–rubella (MMR) vaccine by incorporating the mumps component into the MR vaccine. No case counts or outbreak details are provided in the article.

articles.headlineTranslated: Zocoba to be approved for preventive dosing; world's first pre-onset COVID-19 prophylaxis — Fukushima Minyu Shimbun
articles.summary: Japan's Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare expert panel approved adding preventive dosing to the oral COVID-19 antiviral Zocoba, enabling pre-onset prophylaxis for individuals at risk of infection after exposure (e.g., cohabitants of a confirmed case). If finalized, Zocoba would become the world's first COVID-19 oral medicine with pre-exposure/preventive use to avert illness; the panel noted that preventive dosing already exists for several influenza antivirals. In the same meeting, the panel also approved manufacturing and selling a three-in-one measles-rubella-mumps (MMR) vaccine by combining the MR vaccine with the mumps component. These actions reflect Japan’s progress in expanding prophylactic strategies for infectious diseases and enhancing combination vaccination options.

articles.headlineTranslated: A man in his 40s living in Gifu City has contracted measles; the first measles infection of the year confirmed in Gifu Prefecture (March 2, 2026) | Chukyo TV News NNN
articles.summary: A man in his 40s living in Gifu City has been confirmed to have measles, the first measles infection confirmed in Gifu Prefecture this year. He traveled to Southeast Asia from February 10 to 13 and developed fever after returning on February 24. On February 23, between 5:00 and 6:00 p.m., he visited the Welcia Gifu Sagiyama store, creating potential exposure for others at that time. The city urges anyone who shopped at that store during that window and who develops measles-like symptoms to contact the nearest public health center promptly. Vaccination is emphasized as an effective preventive measure. This case reflects travel-associated exposure and the need for timely reporting and vaccination to prevent wider spread in Gifu Prefecture.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles Alert (February 16–22) | Iwate Nippo Online
articles.summary: Measles alert for Iwate Prefecture, Japan, covering Feb 16–22, reports 1 measles case during the period, bringing the year’s total to 4. Patients present with fever around 38°C, cough, and nasal discharge, followed by a secondary high fever and rash after a temporary improvement. Measles is transmitted by airborne, droplet, and contact routes and is highly contagious. Those with fever and rash should call ahead to a healthcare facility before visiting. The MR vaccine (measles-rubella) is part of routine immunization at 1 year of age and again before entering elementary school; individuals not up to date are advised to consider vaccination.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles infection announced for a Tokyo man in his 20s who visited three udon shops between 10:30 a.m. and around 1 p.m.—"There is a possibility he had contact with a large number of unspecified people"
articles.summary: Measles, laboratory-confirmed, diagnosed in a Tokyo man in his 20s who visited three udon shops in Kagawa Prefecture on Feb 12. He dined at Takamatsu’s Teuchi Judan Udon Baka Ichidai (10:30–11:00), Gamō Udon in Sakaide (11:45–12:15), and Yamagoe Udon in Ayagawa Town (12:30–13:00). He returned to Tokyo by private car; no public transport used. He developed fever, rash, and cough on Feb 13. Tokyo public health authorities reported the case on Feb 24. Kagawa Prefecture warned that the three shops potentially exposed a large number of unspecified customers and staff, advising anyone with fever or rash to seek medical attention per a provider’s instructions. The infection source is not determined; no additional cases are noted in the article.

articles.headlineTranslated: [Details] Man with measles may have come into contact with a large number of unspecified people on the Fukuoka City Subway; if symptoms develop, contact a medical facility and seek a medical examination (Posted February 27, 2026) | FBS NEWS NNN.
articles.summary: Measles diagnosed in a man in his 40s in Fukuoka City, Japan. The patient has no overseas travel history and unknown vaccination status. He traveled by air between Haneda (Tokyo) and Fukuoka on February 20 (JAL313, Haneda→Fukuoka) and February 21 (JAL322, Fukuoka→Haneda), and moved around Fukuoka by subway on February 20–21, visiting the Fukuoka Airport area, Nakasu-Kawabata, Hakata, and Hakata Airport stations at specified times. Symptoms began with fever, rash, cough, runny nose, and conjunctival injection; after 2–4 days of fever, high fever (≥39°C) and rash developed. The period when the patient was potentially infectious overlapped with contact with a large number of unspecified people on public transport. People who used the listed facilities on those dates should monitor for symptoms and, if measles is suspected, contact a medical facility in advance and inform them; avoid using public transit for care. Authorities note no current infection risk from the facilities themselves; no inquiries needed.

articles.headlineTranslated: First measles case of the year confirmed in Kurume City, Fukuoka Prefecture; prefecture warns of highly contagious infection.
articles.summary: On February 26, 2026, Fukuoka Prefecture announced the first measles case in the prefecture this year, in Kurume City. One patient at a Kurume City medical facility had a history of international travel, indicating an imported case. Measles is caused by the measles virus and is highly contagious, spreading via airborne transmission, droplets, and contact. There is no specific antiviral treatment; care is supportive, and vaccination remains the only preventive measure. Because measles is highly transmissible, the prefecture urges anyone with suspected infection to first call a medical facility before visiting. The notice cites the Fukuoka Prefecture Infectious Disease Occurrence Weekly Report and a Kurume City advisory warning residents to be vigilant about measles. Published February 27, 2026.

articles.headlineTranslated: First measles case of the year confirmed in Fukuoka; if you develop symptoms that may indicate measles, call your medical facility; "Influenza Warning" for 14 weeks in a row (Posted February 26, 2026) | FBS NEWS NNN
articles.summary: As of 2026-02-26, Fukuoka Prefecture reported a dip in influenza activity for the week ending February 22, with an average of 42.75 influenza cases per medical facility, down 0.86-fold from the previous week and marking the first decrease in six weeks. Despite the drop, officials have maintained an influenza warning for 14 consecutive weeks and urged continued hygiene measures: handwashing, cough etiquette, and mask use. Separately, the prefecture confirmed the first measles case of the year in Kurume City, reported by a local medical facility. The health department asks residents with fever and rash suspicious for measles to call a medical facility by phone and follow instructions before visiting to ensure proper infection control and care. No further case details are provided.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles-infected man in his 40s living in Tokyo boards a Japan Airlines flight operating a round trip between Haneda and Fukuoka airports; may have come into contact with an unspecified number of people.
articles.summary: Measles infection confirmed in a Tokyo resident in his 40s. He traveled on Japan Airlines flight 313 from Haneda to Fukuoka on Feb 20 morning and flight 322 from Fukuoka to Haneda on Feb 21 evening, potentially exposing an unspecified number of passengers. The patient had no overseas travel history; he first sought medical care on Feb 19 for fever, with PCR confirming measles on Feb 24. Measles is highly contagious; unimmune individuals exposed to an infected person nearly always contract it. Tokyo Metropolitan Government advised passengers on the same flights to seek medical care if rash, runny nose, or red eyes develop. Domestic measles cases have risen since last year; authorities urge those feeling unwell, especially with fever, to stay home. Current date reference: Feb 26, 2026.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles infection spreads to 12 people at the same high school in the Higashi-Mikawa region of Aichi Prefecture.
articles.summary: Twelve teenage male students at a prefectural high school in Toyokawa City, Higashi-Mikawa region, Aichi Prefecture, have laboratory-confirmed measles as of Feb 23. Onset began around Feb 8 with fever, cough, and rash. The school has closed first- and second-year classes since Feb 16, and desks and chairs were disinfected ahead of the Feb 25 high-school entrance exam. The prefectural Infectious Disease Control Division and Toyohashi City have published on their websites the facilities used by students during the exposure period to alert others. This year’s measles cases in the prefecture total 14, approaching last year’s 19. Health authorities urge vaccination and advise anyone with symptoms to contact a healthcare facility before visiting.

articles.summary: Measles infection confirmed in a woman in her 20s in Osaka Prefecture, with lab confirmation reported after a Feb 21 medical facility notification. The Izumi Public Health Center disclosed facilities visited around onset and issued a warning: through Mar 5, anyone developing fever or rash compatible with measles who may have been in contact should contact a health center or medical facility in advance, wear a mask, avoid public transport, and seek prompt care. On Feb 12, the patient visited Drug Akakabe Izumiotsu Showa-cho store in Izumiotsu City from about 6:30–6:40 PM. The virus is described as surviving in air for less than two hours, so there is no infection risk from facilities the patient used at this time. As of Feb 22, Osaka Prefecture has eight confirmed measles cases this year.

articles.headlineTranslated: Measles infection in a man in his 20s may have had contact with an unspecified number of people at the Omori Regional Office in Ota Ward and nearby shopping facilities... He also used the JR Keihin-Tohoku Line.
articles.summary: Measles infection confirmed in a man in his 20s in Yokohama City, Japan. Authorities traced possible exposure on February 10 at the Omori Regional Government Office in Ota Ward and nearby shopping facilities. The patient traveled between Yokohama and Omori Station on the JR Keihin-Tohoku Line, staying on the Omori office’s second floor from 9:00 to 12:00 and 12:50 to 17:00, and eating lunch at the 2nd-floor food court of Machino Omori around 12:10–12:40. He developed fever and rash on February 11 and was confirmed with measles on February 18. He had no overseas travel history. Tokyo Metropolitan Government and city authorities are advising anyone who visited the two facilities on February 10 to monitor for symptoms and seek medical care if changes occur. Officials note domestic measles cases have increased since last year and urge ill individuals to stay home.

As of February 19, 2026, Osaka Prefecture has 7 measles cases this year; nationwide total 32.

articles.headlineTranslated: Five students at a prefectural high school in Aichi Prefecture infected with measles; no overseas travel history.
articles.summary: Five male students at a prefectural high school in East Mikawa, Aichi Prefecture, have measles with no overseas travel history. Of the five, four had vaccination history and one did not. The patient from Toyokawa City was reported on February 16; symptoms began after February 9 (fever, cough, conjunctival injection, eye discharge, rash) and he sought medical care on February 14. On February 16, Toyokawa Public Health Center submitted a measles outbreak notification, and genetic testing at the prefectural health laboratory confirmed measles. The cases are linked to the same high school, indicating domestic transmission within Aichi. Authorities in Aichi Prefecture and Toyohashi City have published information to raise awareness and prevent further spread.

articles.headlineTranslated: Two new infections confirmed; five students at a high school in Higashi-Mikawa, Aichi Prefecture infected with measles; the prefecture urges preventive measures such as vaccination (posted February 18, 2026) | Chukyo TV News NNN
articles.summary: Two additional measles infections were confirmed on February 18, 2026, in male high school students living in Tahara City and Toyohashi City, both attending the same high school in Higashi-Mikawa, Aichi Prefecture. articles.headlineTranslated: Measles (rubeola) patients identified; use of public transportation such as the Joetsu Shinkansen during the period when they could have infected others confirmed [Niigata] (published February 13, 2026) | TeNY NEWS NNN
articles.summary: Measles (rubeola) confirmed in Niigata City: a man in his 30s tested positive at the Niigata City Public Health Center after fever, sore throat, cough and rash on February 1. He has recovered and was discharged, but investigators identified potential exposure on public transport during the contagious period: January 31, 18:12, Joetsu Shinkansen Toki 337 bound for Niigata, car 10; and around 20:10 January 31, a Niigata Kotsu bus from Niigata Station to Furumachi. People without measles immunity who rode these services during the same window could be infected. The three‑week monitoring window extends to February 21, 2026; seek medical advice if fever or rash develops. Measles incubation is about 10 days, with initial fever and cough followed by high fever and rash. In 2025, 265 nationwide cases; Niigata Prefecture reported 4 cases this year. Hotline: Niigata City Public Health Center Infectious Disease Countermeasures Office, 025‑212‑8123 (Feb 14–15, 9:00–17:00).

articles.headline: 県内で7年ぶり感染確認「はしか」どんな病気？（2026年2月13日掲載）｜TVI NEWS NNN
articles.headlineTranslated: Measles: Infection Confirmed in the Prefecture for the First Time in Seven Years — What Kind of Disease Is It? (Posted February 13, 2026) | TVI NEWS NNN
articles.summary: Measles infection confirmed in Iwate Prefecture for the first time in seven years (since 2019). Nationally, measles outbreaks continue with 265 cases reported last year, about six times higher than the previous year; as of February 1, 2026, 23 cases have been confirmed domestically this year. The Japanese Ministry of Health, Labour and Welfare (MHLW) notes measles is caused by the measles virus and spreads via airborne, droplet, and contact transmission; one case in a susceptible population can lead to 10–20 infections. Initial symptoms resemble a cold with fever and cough for 2–3 days, followed by a fever above 39°C and rash. Complications include pneumonia and otitis media; encephalitis occurs in about 0.1%. In pregnancy, infection may cause miscarriage or preterm birth. Vaccination is effective; those never infected or vaccinated should consult their physician. Overseas measles outbreaks since 2023 have increased domestic cases.

articles.headlineTranslated: Influenza Ravaging Osaka; This Season's Second Breach of the "Alert Level"; First Since 1999; Reinfection Risk Is.
articles.summary: Osaka Prefecture announced on February 12 that influenza activity again surpassed the season’s alert level, marking the season’s second breach and the first recurrence since 1999. A survey of 290 medical facilities shows an average of 31.05 influenza patients per facility for February 2–8. After a November 10–16 spike above the threshold of 30, numbers dropped below 10 before rising again in late January. Initially influenza A dominated; influenza B now accounts for a rising share, suggesting reinfection risk for people previously infected. Authorities urge continued infection-control measures, including handwashing and mask use. Separately, measles cases are increasing in Osaka Prefecture, with vaccination urged to curb spread.

articles.headline: 千葉市の２０代男性がはしか感染...都市モノレールやＪＲ京葉線を利用
articles.headlineTranslated: Measles infection in a man in his 20s from Chiba City... who used the Chiba Urban Monorail and JR Keiyo Line.
articles.summary: Measles infection confirmed in a man in his 20s residing in Chiba City, Japan. Chiba City announced on February 10 that the patient, a resident in his 20s, developed fever on February 3, followed by rash after fever subsided; he sought medical care and was laboratory-confirmed positive for measles on February 10. The patient traveled on February 2 using the Chiba Urban Monorail between Sports Center Station and Chiba-Minato Station, and the JR Keiyo Line between Chiba-Minato Station and Maihama Station. This is a single laboratory-confirmed local measles case; public health authorities are conducting contact tracing related to the mentioned transit routes. Public health authorities continue to identify and notify close contacts.
